page: Amazon Web Services m7a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX-512 vector extensions, cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI, and virtualization.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and top-tier results in PassMark extended instructions. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 196.87 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 124.3 GB/sec for uncached large-block writes. In database operations, the server demonstrates top-tier efficiency with 115.33 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and strong PassMark database performance. For machine learning workloads, prompt processing speeds for small LLM models reach top-tier levels at 3786.22 tokens per second, while text generation for medium models remains strong at 36.0 tokens per second.

The instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage or GPU accelerators, meaning workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or hardware-accelerated graphics must rely on network-attached resources. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Given its balanced resource density and high-performance DDR5 memory, the m7a.12xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise applications requiring high memory bandwidth and multi-core processing without the overhead of dedicated GPUs. It is suited for workloads such as database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU capabilities and Redis database operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth and software compilation speeds. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory throughput and balanced compute resources. It is well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 19 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-performance general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding multi-core and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and Redis database benchmark performance
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and web serving
